sections: Canonical artifacts, renderer-derived views, and Projections
Intake record and artifact candidate contracts
PR-009 — Charter questionnaire-shaped coverage and posture validation
complete selected dependency and transition record

repo_truth: Fresh read-only review of the exact eleven-entry HCM-2.2 remediated planning subject. The subject remains planning-only and proposes future authority; no HCM-2.2 product or definition byte has landed. Revalidate the complete packet, literal contract fixtures, proof, live definitions and carriers, and implementability without inheriting any prior clean conclusion.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted eleven-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of 00, 04, 06, the immutable Review 1 dispatch, HCM-2.2 SPEC, both contract fixtures, plan, unchecked todo, planning proof, and remediation record.
Read unchanged 01, 02, 03, 05, 07, 08, HCM-0.6 decision, selected HCM-2.1 record/packet/proof, exact definition assets, and live engine/compiler/flow/CLI/skill/template/test sources only as needed.

Run bounded read-only manifest, Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, exact-ref/fingerprint-cycle, source/API feasibility, field-preservation, lineage/authority/transaction, byte-contract, cross-platform, determinism, security, bridge, and classification checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.

No Rust, Cargo, definition, template, fixture outside the subject, test, runtime/product, generated-skill, pipeline, HCM-2.3+, Phase-3, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, contract, dock, or publication implementation.

Do not invent an alternate schema, intake, CLI, definition cycle, authority/authentication claim, storage transaction, lifecycle, renderer, profile, migration, compatibility mode, trigger, bridge, or sibling-family scope.
Do not treat prior review history, remediation wording, baseline tests, literal fixtures, or contingent status as proof that the eleven-entry subject is clean.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact eleven-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.
Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order with exact file/line, violated authority, failure, smallest remediation, and missing or invalidated proof.

Verify every new definition record and fingerprint dependency is exact, acyclic, meta-schema implementable, old-byte preserving, and selected only by its permitted owner; especially kind independence from intake and renderer independence from kind fingerprint.

Verify every retained rich Charter field/validation semantic has one closed canonical carrier and coverage mapping, and the new validator/version/capability bindings are total without mutating released refs.
Verify bootstrap/amendment authority, waiver eligibility, immutable lineage, promotion journal commit point/recovery/reader gating, platform refusal, and proof matrices are honest and implementable.

Verify canonical YAML grammar and literal fixture, Markdown matrix and literal fixture, sizes/hashes, independent implementations, fingerprints, lifecycle, selected consumers, C03/C04, bridges, and deletion gates are total and non-circular.
Verify planning-only status, exact scope, unchecked todo, proof counts, gate/classification ceiling, CRITICAL GitNexus warning, primary commit, and separate parent handoff/ledger closeout.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live definitions/source, literal fixtures, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.

Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than selecting unspecified semantic bytes, authority behavior, transaction visibility, unsafe platform fallback, compatibility, pipeline, sibling-family, or later-slice behavior.
Return to the parent if valid remediation would require implementation now, released-definition mutation, broader product/default-set authority, HCM-2.3+, or global handoff/ledger mutation.
